{"key_event":"Mobutu state hollowing and multiparty opening","reasoning":"State power fell sharply as fiscal collapse, Gécamines decline, unpaid predatory soldiers, looting, and loss of control in Kivu reduced administrative and coercive reach, though Mobutu still retained some coercive and patronage levers. Society gained space through opposition parties, churches, unions, human-rights groups, and the Sovereign National Conference, but poverty, repression, army violence, and ethnic conflict kept autonomous civic power weak rather than moderate.","society_change":1.0,"society_power":3.6,"state_change":-1.6,"state_power":3.1}
